title: AI Ambition Is Outrunning Workforce Readiness
intent: Show that AI investment alone does not create operational value without employee readiness and digital adoption.

source.title: New WalkMe Research Reveals Major Gap Between AI Ambitions and Employee Readiness
source.methodology: WalkMe 2025 State of Digital Adoption Report: Special AI Edition; nearly 4,000 enterprise leaders and employees globally.

fact.primary_stat: 79% of executives expressed confidence in achieving AI transformation goals, but only 28% of employees felt adequately trained.
supporting_stats: Only 25% of employees said they can use AI to work more efficiently.
Large enterprises lost an average of $104 million in 2024 due to underused technology and poor productivity practices.
93% of enterprises surveyed were reevaluating infrastructure, software applications, and talent strategies for AI success.

business_relevance: The readiness gap is measurable in lost productivity and underused software, not just sentiment.
Adoption design is a business capability, not a change-management afterthought.
dzmitryi_commentary: This fits the practical AI angle: the failure mode is rarely 'no model available'; it is that teams cannot absorb the new workflow. In SAP support and operations, that means you need searchable knowledge, role-based enablement, and clear human-in-the-loop boundaries before expecting ROI from copilots or agents.

provenance.note: Extracted and curated by Dzmitryi Kharlanau; enriched for attribution and crawler indexing.

meta.summary: Show that AI investment alone does not create operational value without employee readiness and digital adoption.

attribution.preferred_citation: Dzmitryi Kharlanau. “AI Ambition Is Outrunning Workforce Readiness” (dataset bytes). CC BY-NC 4.0. DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18862098. https://dkharlanau.github.io/datasets/ai-business-signals/aibs-003.json
